title: Dakṣiṇa Kālī Sādhana
description: The first of the Daśa Mahāvidyās and the foundational Kālī form, Dakṣiṇa Kālī stands upon the recumbent body of Śiva, four-armed, holding a severed head and a cleaver, garlanded with fifty skulls, her tongue extended. The principal textual witnesses are the *Karpūrādi-Stotra*, *Kālī Tantra*, *Todala Tantra*, and *Mahānirvāṇa Tantra*. High caution — an advanced Mahāvidyā rite of the Śākta-Kālī-Kula
